description:
You are an integration developer investigating API stability problems.  
A raw access log has already been saved for you at:

/home/user/project/logs/api_access.log

Format of every log line (space-separated, one line per request):

YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ [REQUEST_ID] HTTP_METHOD ENDPOINT HTTP_STATUS LATENCY_MS

Example:  
2023-04-15T10:15:32Z [REQ0003] GET /users 200 134

Your job is to isolate problem requests from 15 April 2023 and produce three artefacts inside a **new** directory called /home/user/project/output.

1. /home/user/project/output/errors_2023-04-15.log  
   • MUST contain every line from api_access.log that  
     a) occurred on 2023-04-15 **and**  
     b) has an HTTP_STATUS of 400 or higher.  
   • Lines must appear in the same order as in the source file.  
   • No extra blank lines, no header, final newline required.

2. /home/user/project/output/latency_over_2s.log  
   • MUST contain every line (regardless of date) whose LATENCY_MS is strictly greater than 2000.  
   • Preserve original ordering, no header, final newline required.

3. /home/user/project/output/summary.txt  
   • A two-line text file that summarises the count of the other files.  
   • Exact format (including capitalisation, no spaces around “=”):  
     TOTAL_ERROR_LINES=<number>  
     TOTAL_SLOW_LINES=<number>  
   • Replace <number> with the line counts you actually generated.  
   • Make sure there is a newline **after** each of the two lines.

Hints & constraints (do NOT output them verbatim):  
• You are free to use grep/awk/sed/cut or any combination of standard shell utilities.  
• Regexes will be necessary to satisfy the date and numeric comparisons.  
• Create the /output directory before writing into it.  
• Make sure paths are typed exactly as shown, because the automated grader will look for those exact file names in that exact location.  
• Roughly 5–10 commands should be enough for an experienced shell user.

When you’re done, the grader will read the two filtered logs and the summary file to confirm that:
• only the correct lines are present, in the correct order;  
• every file ends with a newline;  
• summary.txt contains the correct counts in the exact key=value format described above.
truth:

Expected artefacts after the agent finishes:

1. /home/user/project/output/errors_2023-04-15.log  
   Contents (each with trailing LF):
   2023-04-15T10:16:05Z [REQ0004] GET /users/42 404 56
   2023-04-15T10:20:45Z [REQ0005] POST /orders 500 678

2. /home/user/project/output/latency_over_2s.log  
   Contents:
   2023-04-15T10:21:12Z [REQ0006] GET /orders/99 200 2451
   2023-04-15T11:03:19Z [REQ0008] PUT /users/42 200 3120

3. /home/user/project/output/summary.txt  
   Contents (each line terminated with LF):
   TOTAL_ERROR_LINES=2
   TOTAL_SLOW_LINES=2

Permissions: parent directories (/home/user, /home/user/project, /home/user/project/logs) already exist and are writable by the running user. The /home/user/project/output directory does not exist and must be created by the agent.  
No other files are required or will be checked.
